full_scripture: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, If any man of you bring an offering unto the LORD, ye shall bring your offering of the cattle, even of the herd, and of the flock.
speaker: God
conditions: when presenting a burnt offering unto the LORD
command_summary: Bring offerings from the herd or flock
interpretation_notes: This establishes the acceptable categories of animals for burnt offerings under the Mosaic covenant.

full_scripture: If his offering be a burnt sacrifice of the herd, let him offer a male without blemish.
speaker: God
conditions: if presenting a burnt offering from the herd
command_summary: Offer a male without blemish
interpretation_notes: The requirement of being without blemish symbolizes purity and acceptability before God.

full_scripture: He shall offer it of his own voluntary will at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation before the LORD.
speaker: God
command_summary: Present the offering voluntarily before the LORD
interpretation_notes: The voluntary aspect emphasizes willing worship and covenant devotion.

full_scripture: And he shall put his hand upon the head of the burnt offering.
speaker: God
conditions: before the burnt offering is accepted
command_summary: Lay hands upon the burnt offering
interpretation_notes: The laying on of hands represents identification with the sacrifice for atonement.

full_scripture: And he shall kill the bullock before the LORD: and the priests, Aaron's sons, shall bring the blood, and sprinkle the blood round about upon the altar.
speaker: God
command_summary: Kill the bullock and sprinkle the blood on the altar
interpretation_notes: The priests mediate the sacrificial process through blood application at the altar.

full_scripture: And he shall flay the burnt offering, and cut it into his pieces.
speaker: God
command_summary: Skin and divide the burnt offering
interpretation_notes: The preparation of the sacrifice follows an ordered ritual structure.

full_scripture: And the sons of Aaron the priest shall put fire upon the altar, and lay the wood in order upon the fire.
speaker: God
conditions: during preparation of the burnt offering
command_summary: Prepare the altar fire and arrange the wood
interpretation_notes: The priests are responsible for properly preparing the altar for sacrifice.

full_scripture: And the priests, Aaron's sons, shall lay the parts, the head, and the fat, in order upon the wood that is on the fire which is upon the altar:
speaker: God
conditions: after the sacrifice has been divided into pieces
command_summary: Arrange the sacrifice upon the altar
interpretation_notes: The sacrifice is carefully arranged before being consumed on the altar.

full_scripture: But his inwards and his legs shall he wash in water: and the priest shall burn all on the altar, to be a burnt sacrifice, an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the LORD.
speaker: God
conditions: before the burnt offering is fully consumed
command_summary: Wash and burn the whole offering on the altar
interpretation_notes: The washing symbolizes ritual purity before the offering ascends to God.

full_scripture: And if his offering be of the flocks, namely, of the sheep, or of the goats, for a burnt sacrifice; he shall bring it a male without blemish.
speaker: God
conditions: if presenting a burnt offering from the flock
command_summary: Offer a spotless male from the flock
interpretation_notes: The same purity standard applies to sheep and goats as to herd offerings.

full_scripture: And he shall kill it on the side of the altar northward before the LORD: and the priests, Aaron's sons, shall sprinkle his blood round about upon the altar.
speaker: God
conditions: during the flock burnt offering ritual
command_summary: Kill the offering and sprinkle the blood
interpretation_notes: The blood ritual remains central regardless of the sacrificial animal.

full_scripture: And he shall cut it into his pieces, with his head and his fat: and the priest shall lay them in order on the wood that is on the fire which is upon the altar:
speaker: God
command_summary: Cut and arrange the sacrifice on the altar
interpretation_notes: The ritual structure mirrors the earlier herd offering instructions.

full_scripture: But he shall wash the inwards and the legs with water: and the priest shall bring it all, and burn it upon the altar: it is a burnt sacrifice, an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the LORD.
speaker: God
conditions: before the flock offering is completely burned
command_summary: Wash and fully burn the flock offering
interpretation_notes: The offering is described as a pleasing aroma unto the LORD.

full_scripture: And if the burnt sacrifice for his offering to the LORD be of fowls, then he shall bring his offering of turtledoves, or of young pigeons.
speaker: God
conditions: if presenting a bird burnt offering
command_summary: Offer turtledoves or young pigeons
interpretation_notes: Bird offerings provided a sacrificial option accessible to poorer worshipers.

full_scripture: And the priest shall bring it unto the altar, and wring off his head, and burn it on the altar; and the blood thereof shall be wrung out at the side of the altar:
speaker: God
command_summary: Prepare and burn the bird offering
interpretation_notes: Even the smallest offerings required priestly mediation and blood application.

full_scripture: And he shall pluck away his crop with his feathers, and cast it beside the altar on the east part, by the place of the ashes:
speaker: God
command_summary: Remove the crop and cast it beside the altar
interpretation_notes: Impure or unsuitable portions are removed before offering the sacrifice.

full_scripture: And he shall cleave it with the wings thereof, but shall not divide it asunder: and the priest shall burn it upon the altar, upon the wood that is upon the fire: it is a burnt sacrifice, an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the LORD.
speaker: God
conditions: during the completion of the bird burnt offering
command_summary: Burn the bird offering upon the altar
interpretation_notes: The bird offering concludes with the same description of pleasing acceptance before the LORD.

full_scripture: And when any will offer a meat offering unto the LORD, his offering shall be of fine flour; and he shall pour oil upon it, and put frankincense thereon:
speaker: God
conditions: when presenting a meat offering unto the LORD
command_summary: Prepare a grain offering with flour, oil, and frankincense
interpretation_notes: The grain offering is presented as a tribute of dedication and thanksgiving unto the LORD.

full_scripture: And he shall bring it to Aaron's sons the priests: and he shall take thereout his handful of the flour thereof, and of the oil thereof, with all the frankincense thereof; and the priest shall burn the memorial of it upon the altar.
speaker: God
command_summary: Burn a memorial portion upon the altar
interpretation_notes: Only a memorial portion is burned while the remainder belongs to the priests.

full_scripture: And the remnant of the meat offering shall be Aaron's and his sons': it is a thing most holy of the offerings of the LORD made by fire.
speaker: God
conditions: after the memorial portion is burned
command_summary: The remaining grain offering belongs to the priests
interpretation_notes: The priests are sustained through portions of the sacrificial system.

full_scripture: And if thou bring an oblation of a meat offering baken in the oven, it shall be unleavened cakes of fine flour mingled with oil, or unleavened wafers anointed with oil.
speaker: God
conditions: if presenting a baked grain offering
command_summary: Prepare unleavened baked grain offerings
interpretation_notes: Leaven is excluded from offerings associated with holiness and purity.

full_scripture: And if thy oblation be a meat offering baken in a pan, it shall be of fine flour unleavened, mingled with oil.
speaker: God
conditions: if presenting a pan-cooked grain offering
command_summary: Prepare unleavened grain offerings in a pan
interpretation_notes: Different preparation methods are permitted while maintaining purity requirements.

full_scripture: Thou shalt part it in pieces, and pour oil thereon: it is a meat offering.
speaker: God
conditions: after preparing the pan-cooked grain offering
command_summary: Divide the offering and pour oil upon it
interpretation_notes: Oil consistently accompanies grain offerings as part of consecrated worship.

full_scripture: And if thy oblation be a meat offering baken in the fryingpan, it shall be made of fine flour with oil.
speaker: God
conditions: if presenting a frying-pan grain offering
command_summary: Prepare grain offerings with flour and oil
interpretation_notes: The offering regulations adapt to varying preparation styles while preserving sacred standards.

full_scripture: And thou shalt bring the meat offering that is made of these things unto the LORD: and when it is presented unto the priest, he shall bring it unto the altar.
speaker: God
command_summary: Present the grain offering to the priest and altar
interpretation_notes: Priestly mediation remains central in presenting offerings before the LORD.

full_scripture: And the priest shall take from the meat offering a memorial thereof, and shall burn it upon the altar: it is an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the LORD.
speaker: God
command_summary: Burn the memorial portion on the altar
interpretation_notes: The offering ascends as a pleasing aroma before the LORD.

full_scripture: And that which is left of the meat offering shall be Aaron's and his sons': it is a thing most holy of the offerings of the LORD made by fire.
speaker: God
conditions: after the memorial portion is burned
command_summary: The remaining grain offering belongs to the priests
interpretation_notes: The priests receive holy portions as part of covenant worship provision.

full_scripture: No meat offering, which ye shall bring unto the LORD, shall be made with leaven: for ye shall burn no leaven, nor any honey, in any offering of the LORD made by fire.
speaker: God
conditions: when presenting offerings made by fire unto the LORD
command_summary: Do not offer leaven or honey in offerings by fire
interpretation_notes: Leaven and honey are excluded from altar offerings due to their association with fermentation and corruption.

full_scripture: As for the oblation of the firstfruits, ye shall offer them unto the LORD: but they shall not be burnt on the altar for a sweet savour.
speaker: God
command_summary: Offer firstfruits but do not burn them on the altar
interpretation_notes: Firstfruit offerings are holy unto the LORD but are handled differently from altar sacrifices.

full_scripture: And every oblation of thy meat offering shalt thou season with salt; neither shalt thou suffer the salt of the covenant of thy God to be lacking from thy meat offering: with all thine offerings thou shalt offer salt.
speaker: God
conditions: with every grain offering and sacrifice
command_summary: Season every offering with salt
interpretation_notes: Salt symbolizes covenant permanence, preservation, and faithfulness before God.

full_scripture: And if thou offer a meat offering of thy firstfruits unto the LORD, thou shalt offer for the meat offering of thy firstfruits green ears of corn dried by the fire, even corn beaten out of full ears.
speaker: God
conditions: if presenting a firstfruit grain offering
command_summary: Offer roasted firstfruit grain unto the LORD
interpretation_notes: The first and best produce of the harvest is dedicated unto the LORD.

full_scripture: And thou shalt put oil upon it, and lay frankincense thereon: it is a meat offering.
speaker: God
conditions: when preparing a firstfruit grain offering
command_summary: Add oil and frankincense to the offering
interpretation_notes: Oil and frankincense remain consistent symbols of consecration and acceptable worship.

full_scripture: And the priest shall burn the memorial of it, part of the beaten corn thereof, and part of the oil thereof, with all the frankincense thereof: it is an offering made by fire unto the LORD.
speaker: God
conditions: during the firstfruit grain offering ritual
command_summary: Burn the memorial portion of the firstfruit offering
interpretation_notes: The memorial burning signifies dedication of the harvest unto the LORD.

full_scripture: And if his oblation be a sacrifice of peace offering, if he offer it of the herd; whether it be a male or female, he shall offer it without blemish before the LORD.
speaker: God
conditions: if presenting a peace offering from the herd
command_summary: Offer a spotless peace offering from the herd
interpretation_notes: Peace offerings required unblemished animals as acceptable worship before the LORD.

full_scripture: And he shall lay his hand upon the head of his offering, and kill it at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation: and Aaron's sons the priests shall sprinkle the blood upon the altar round about.
speaker: God
command_summary: Lay hands on the offering and sprinkle the blood
interpretation_notes: The laying on of hands and blood application identify the worshiper with the sacrifice.

full_scripture: And he shall offer of the sacrifice of the peace offering an offering made by fire unto the LORD; the fat that covereth the inwards, and all the fat that is upon the inwards... and Aaron's sons shall burn it on the altar upon the burnt sacrifice.
speaker: God
command_summary: Offer the fat portions upon the altar
interpretation_notes: The choicest portions belong to the LORD and are burned upon the altar.

full_scripture: And if his offering for a sacrifice of peace offering unto the LORD be of the flock; male or female, he shall offer it without blemish.
speaker: God
conditions: if presenting a peace offering from the flock
command_summary: Offer a spotless peace offering from the flock
interpretation_notes: The requirement for an unblemished offering applies equally to flock sacrifices.

full_scripture: If he offer a lamb for his offering, then shall he offer it before the LORD... and the priest shall burn it upon the altar: it is the food of the offering made by fire unto the LORD.
speaker: God
conditions: if presenting a lamb peace offering
command_summary: Offer and burn the lamb peace offering
interpretation_notes: The lamb peace offering follows the same sacrificial structure while specifying lamb-related portions.

full_scripture: And if his offering be a goat, then he shall offer it before the LORD... and the priest shall burn them upon the altar: it is the food of the offering made by fire for a sweet savour.
speaker: God
conditions: if presenting a goat peace offering
command_summary: Offer and burn the goat peace offering
interpretation_notes: The goat offering maintains the pattern of blood application and burning of the fat portions.

full_scripture: It shall be a perpetual statute for your generations throughout all your dwellings, that ye eat neither fat nor blood.
speaker: God
conditions: throughout Israel's generations under the Mosaic covenant
command_summary: Do not eat fat or blood
interpretation_notes: The prohibition on consuming blood and sacrificial fat distinguishes holy things reserved unto God.

full_scripture: Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, If a soul shall sin through ignorance against any of the commandments of the LORD concerning things which ought not to be done...
speaker: God
conditions: when a sin of ignorance is committed
command_summary: Instructions for unintentional sin offerings
interpretation_notes: Introduces the sin offering system for unintentional transgression.

full_scripture: If the priest that is anointed do sin according to the sin of the people...
speaker: God
command_summary: Sin offering required for priestly sin
interpretation_notes: Establishes higher accountability for priestly sin.

full_scripture: He shall bring the bullock unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation before the LORD...
speaker: God
command_summary: Bring a bull to the tabernacle
interpretation_notes: Priestly sin requires a costly offering at the tabernacle entrance.

full_scripture: And the priest that is anointed shall take of the bullock's blood, and bring it to the tabernacle of the congregation...
speaker: God
command_summary: Handle and bring blood into the tabernacle
interpretation_notes: Blood manipulation signifies atonement process beginning.

full_scripture: And the priest shall dip his finger in the blood, and sprinkle of the blood seven times before the LORD...
speaker: God
command_summary: Sprinkle blood before the LORD
interpretation_notes: Sevenfold sprinkling symbolizes complete purification.

full_scripture: And the priest shall put some of the blood upon the horns of the altar of sweet incense...
speaker: God
command_summary: Apply blood to altar horns
interpretation_notes: Blood application sanctifies the altar of incense.

full_scripture: And he shall take off from it all the fat of the bullock for the sin offering...
speaker: God
command_summary: Remove fat portions for offering
interpretation_notes: Specific portions are designated for burning to the LORD.

full_scripture: And the fat thereof, and the kidneys, and the caul above the liver...
speaker: God
command_summary: Burn specific internal portions
interpretation_notes: Internal fat portions represent the choicest parts dedicated to God.

full_scripture: As it was taken off from the bullock of the sacrifice of peace offerings...
speaker: God
command_summary: Follow peace offering fat removal pattern
interpretation_notes: Sin offering procedure references peace offering structure.

full_scripture: And the skin of the bull, and all his flesh, with his head, and with his legs, and his inwards, and his dung, even the whole bull shall he carry forth without the camp unto a clean place, where the ashes are poured out, and burn him on the wood with fire: where the ashes are poured out shall he be burnt.
speaker: God
conditions: after sin offering for the anointed priest
command_summary: Burn the bull outside the camp
interpretation_notes: The removal outside the camp signifies removal of sin from the covenant community.

full_scripture: Even the whole bull shall he carry forth without the camp unto a clean place, and burn him there on the wood with fire: where the ashes are poured out shall he be burnt.
speaker: God
command_summary: Burn the sin offering in a clean place outside the camp
interpretation_notes: The repetition emphasizes complete removal of impurity from the camp.

full_scripture: And if the whole congregation of Israel sin through ignorance, and the thing be hid from the eyes of the assembly, and they have done somewhat against any of the commandments of the LORD concerning things which should not be done, and are guilty;
speaker: God
conditions: if the whole congregation sins unintentionally
command_summary: If the congregation sins unintentionally
interpretation_notes: This introduces corporate guilt requiring atonement.

full_scripture: When the sin, which they have sinned against it, is known, then the congregation shall offer a young bullock for the sin, and bring him before the tabernacle of the congregation.
speaker: God
command_summary: Offer a bullock for corporate sin
interpretation_notes: Corporate sin requires a collective atoning sacrifice.

full_scripture: And the elders of the congregation shall lay their hands upon the head of the bullock before the LORD: and the bullock shall be killed before the LORD.
speaker: God
command_summary: Elders lay hands and kill the bullock
interpretation_notes: Leadership represents the people in confession and atonement.

full_scripture: And the priest that is anointed shall bring of the bullock’s blood to the tabernacle of the congregation:
speaker: God
command_summary: Bring the blood to the tabernacle
interpretation_notes: Blood mediation is central to atonement ritual.

full_scripture: And the priest shall dip his finger in some of the blood, and sprinkle it seven times before the LORD, even before the veil.
speaker: God
command_summary: Sprinkle blood before the LORD
interpretation_notes: Sevenfold sprinkling signifies completeness of atonement.

full_scripture: And he shall put some of the blood upon the horns of the altar which is before the LORD, that is in the tabernacle of the congregation, and shall pour out all the blood at the bottom of the altar of the burnt offering, which is at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.
speaker: God
command_summary: Apply blood to altar horns and pour remainder at base
interpretation_notes: Blood manipulation completes purification of the altar space.

full_scripture: And he shall take all his fat from him, and burn it upon the altar.
speaker: God
command_summary: Burn the fat on the altar
interpretation_notes: Fat represents the richest portion dedicated to God.

full_scripture: And he shall do with the bullock as he did with the bullock for a sin offering, so shall he do with this: and the priest shall make an atonement for them, and it shall be forgiven them.
speaker: God
command_summary: Priest makes atonement and forgiveness is granted
interpretation_notes: Atonement results in divine forgiveness.

full_scripture: And he shall carry forth the bullock without the camp, and burn him as he burned the first bullock: it is a sin offering for the congregation.
speaker: God
command_summary: Burn the bull outside the camp
interpretation_notes: Removal outside the camp signifies removal of sin from the community.

full_scripture: When a ruler hath sinned, and done somewhat through ignorance against any of the commandments of the LORD his God concerning things which should not be done, and is guilty;
speaker: God
command_summary: Provision for sin of a ruler
interpretation_notes: Introduces sin offering category for leaders.

full_scripture: Or if his sin, wherein he hath sinned, come to his knowledge; he shall bring his offering, a kid of the goats, a male without blemish:
speaker: God
command_summary: Bring a male goat without blemish
interpretation_notes: Leader held to same purity standards as community.

full_scripture: And he shall lay his hand upon the head of the goat, and kill it in the place where they kill the burnt offering before the LORD: it is a sin offering.
speaker: God
command_summary: Lay hand on goat and kill it
interpretation_notes: Leader participates directly in sacrificial identification.

full_scripture: And the priest shall take of the blood of the sin offering with his finger, and put it upon the horns of the altar of burnt offering, and shall pour out his blood at the bottom of the altar of burnt offering.
speaker: God
command_summary: Apply blood to altar horns and base
interpretation_notes: Same priestly blood ritual as earlier offerings.

full_scripture: And he shall burn all his fat upon the altar, as the fat of the sacrifice of peace offerings: and the priest shall make an atonement for him as concerning his sin, and it shall be forgiven him.
speaker: God
command_summary: Atonement brings forgiveness
interpretation_notes: Forgiveness is explicitly tied to completed atonement.

full_scripture: And if any one of the common people sin through ignorance, while he doeth somewhat against any of the commandments of the LORD concerning things which ought not to be done, and be guilty;
speaker: God
conditions: if a common person sins unintentionally
command_summary: Provision for unintentional sin by common people
interpretation_notes: Introduces atonement structure for laypeople similar to leaders but scaled to social status.

full_scripture: Or if his sin, which he hath sinned, come to his knowledge: then he shall bring his offering, a kid of the goats, a female without blemish, for his sin which he hath sinned.
speaker: God
command_summary: Bring a female goat without blemish for sin
interpretation_notes: Emphasizes substitutionary atonement through a specific female animal offering.

full_scripture: And he shall lay his hand upon the head of the sin offering, and slay the sin offering in the place of the burnt offering.
speaker: God
command_summary: Lay hand on and kill the sin offering
interpretation_notes: Identificational transfer of sin precedes execution of sacrifice.

full_scripture: And the priest shall take of the blood thereof with his finger, and put it upon the horns of the altar of burnt offering, and shall pour out all the blood thereof at the bottom of the altar.
speaker: God
command_summary: Apply blood to altar and pour remaining blood out
interpretation_notes: Blood manipulation symbolizes purification and atonement before God.

full_scripture: And he shall take away all the fat thereof, as the fat is taken away from off the sacrifice of peace offerings; and the priest shall burn it upon the altar for a sweet savour unto the LORD.
speaker: God
command_summary: Remove fat and burn it as offering
interpretation_notes: Fat is treated as the portion reserved for burning unto the LORD.

full_scripture: And if he bring a lamb for a sin offering, he shall bring it a female without blemish.
speaker: God
conditions: if a lamb is used for sin offering
command_summary: Offer a female lamb without blemish
interpretation_notes: Allows variation in sacrifice type while maintaining purity requirement.

full_scripture: And he shall lay his hand upon the head of the sin offering, and slay it for a sin offering in the place where they kill the burnt offering.
speaker: God
command_summary: Lay hand and kill sin offering
interpretation_notes: Reinforces substitutionary atonement pattern across animal types.

full_scripture: And the priest shall take of the blood of the sin offering with his finger, and put it upon the horns of the altar of burnt offering, and shall pour out all the blood thereof at the bottom of the altar:
speaker: God
conditions: after slaughter of lamb sin offering
command_summary: Apply and pour out blood at altar
interpretation_notes: Repeats blood ritual pattern emphasizing purification.

full_scripture: And he shall take away all the fat thereof, as the fat of the lamb is taken away from the sacrifice of the peace offerings; and the priest shall burn them upon the altar, according to the offerings made by fire unto the LORD: and the priest shall make an atonement for his sin that he hath committed, and it shall be forgiven him.
speaker: God
command_summary: Atonement is made and forgiveness granted
interpretation_notes: Culmination of sin offering: atonement results in forgiveness.

full_scripture: And if a soul sin, and hear the voice of swearing, and is a witness, whether he hath seen or known of it; if he do not utter it, then he shall bear his iniquity.
speaker: God
conditions: if a witness withholds testimony under oath
command_summary: Do not withhold testimony
interpretation_notes: Silence in legal testimony is treated as participation in guilt.

full_scripture: Or if a soul touch any unclean thing... he also shall be unclean and guilty.
speaker: God
command_summary: Avoid uncleanness or bear guilt
interpretation_notes: Ritual impurity produces guilt when ignored.

full_scripture: Or if a soul swear... and it be hid from him; when he knoweth of it, then he shall be guilty.
speaker: God
command_summary: Unfulfilled oaths result in guilt
interpretation_notes: Speech before God carries binding moral weight.

full_scripture: And it shall be, when he shall be guilty... that he shall confess that he hath sinned.
speaker: God
command_summary: Confess sin when guilty
interpretation_notes: Confession is required for atonement process to begin.

full_scripture: And he shall bring his trespass offering unto the LORD for his sin...
speaker: God
command_summary: Bring a trespass offering
interpretation_notes: Sacrifice follows confession as restitution pathway.

full_scripture: And if he be not able to bring a lamb, then he shall bring two turtledoves, or two young pigeons...
speaker: God
command_summary: Offer birds if unable to afford lamb
interpretation_notes: Provision is made for economic disparity in atonement.

full_scripture: But if he be not able to bring two turtledoves... then he shall bring the tenth part of an ephah of fine flour...
speaker: God
command_summary: Offer fine flour if poor
interpretation_notes: Extreme poverty does not exclude access to atonement.

full_scripture: And he shall bring it to the priest, and the priest shall take his handful of it, even a memorial thereof, and burn it on the altar, according to the offerings made by fire unto the LORD: it is a sin offering.
speaker: God
conditions: when a sin offering is brought
command_summary: Present sin offering and burn a memorial portion
interpretation_notes: The priestly portion functions as a memorial portion burned before the LORD.

full_scripture: And the priest shall make an atonement for him as touching his sin that he hath sinned in one of these, and it shall be forgiven him: and the remnant shall be the priest's, as a meat offering.
speaker: God
command_summary: Atonement is made and forgiveness granted
interpretation_notes: Forgiveness is explicitly tied to priestly atonement.

full_scripture: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying, If a soul commit a trespass, and sin through ignorance in the holy things of the LORD...
speaker: God
conditions: if a trespass is committed unknowingly
command_summary: Instructions for unintentional trespass against holy things
interpretation_notes: Introduces unintentional sin against sacred things.

full_scripture: Then he shall bring for his trespass unto the LORD a ram without blemish out of the flocks...
speaker: God
command_summary: Bring a ram without blemish as trespass offering
interpretation_notes: Unblemished ram required for restitution for sacred violations.

full_scripture: And he shall make amends for the harm that he hath done in the holy thing, and shall add the fifth part thereto...
speaker: God
command_summary: Make restitution plus one-fifth penalty
interpretation_notes: Restitution includes penalty beyond original value.

full_scripture: And if a soul sin, and commit any of these things which are forbidden... though he wist it not, yet is he guilty.
speaker: God
command_summary: Unintentional sin still incurs guilt
interpretation_notes: Ignorance does not remove covenant accountability.

full_scripture: And he shall bring a ram without blemish out of the flock, and the priest shall make an atonement for him concerning his ignorance...
speaker: God
command_summary: Bring ram and receive atonement for ignorance
interpretation_notes: Atonement covers unintentional violations once sacrifice is made.

full_scripture: It is a trespass offering: he hath certainly trespassed against the LORD.
speaker: God
conditions: after recognition of sin and offering
command_summary: Trespass is confirmed against the LORD
interpretation_notes: Final confirmation of guilt under the trespass offering system.

full_scripture: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,
speaker: God
command_summary: Introduction to priestly laws
interpretation_notes: Transition into detailed priestly regulations.

full_scripture: If a soul sin, and commit a trespass against the LORD, and lie unto his neighbour...
speaker: God
conditions: when a person commits fraud or theft
command_summary: Do not commit fraud or deceit
interpretation_notes: Introduces restitution laws for interpersonal sin.

full_scripture: Then it shall be, because he hath sinned, and is guilty, that he shall restore...
speaker: God
command_summary: Restore what was taken
interpretation_notes: Restitution is required in addition to guilt acknowledgment.

full_scripture: Then it shall be, because he hath sinned, that he shall restore that which he took violently away...
speaker: God
conditions: when theft or violence has occurred
command_summary: Restore stolen or unjustly taken property
interpretation_notes: Restitution includes compensation beyond original loss.

full_scripture: He shall even restore it in the principal, and shall add the fifth part more thereto...
speaker: God
command_summary: Restore plus one-fifth penalty
interpretation_notes: Adds penalty to ensure justice and discourage wrongdoing.

full_scripture: And he shall bring his trespass offering unto the LORD...
speaker: God
command_summary: Bring trespass offering to the LORD
interpretation_notes: Spiritual atonement follows material restitution.

full_scripture: And the priest shall make an atonement for him before the LORD...
speaker: God
command_summary: Priest makes atonement
interpretation_notes: Priestly mediation completes the reconciliation process.

full_scripture: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,
speaker: God
conditions: transition to priestly burnt offering law
command_summary: Introduction to burnt offering instructions for priests
interpretation_notes: Marks shift from restitution law back to sacrificial system instruction.

full_scripture: Command Aaron and his sons, saying, This is the law of the burnt offering...
speaker: God
command_summary: Instructions for the burnt offering law
interpretation_notes: Begins detailed priestly maintenance of the daily burnt offering.

full_scripture: The burnt offering shall be on the hearth upon the altar all night unto the morning...
speaker: God
command_summary: Keep the burnt offering burning overnight
interpretation_notes: Emphasizes continual fire symbolizing ongoing devotion.

full_scripture: And the priest shall put on his linen garment, and his linen breeches shall he put upon his flesh...
speaker: God
command_summary: Priest must wear linen garments for service
interpretation_notes: Garments emphasize ritual separation and holiness.

full_scripture: And the fire upon the altar shall be burning in it; it shall not be put out...
speaker: God
command_summary: Keep altar fire continually burning
interpretation_notes: Perpetual fire symbolizes uninterrupted worship and atonement access.

full_scripture: The fire shall ever be burning upon the altar; it shall never go out.
speaker: God
command_summary: Maintain perpetual altar fire
interpretation_notes: Reinforces permanence and continuity of worship system.

full_scripture: And this is the law of the meat offering...
speaker: God
command_summary: Introduction to grain offering law
interpretation_notes: Shifts from burnt offering regulation to grain offering instructions.

full_scripture: And this is the law of the meat offering: the sons of Aaron shall offer it before the LORD, before the altar.
speaker: God
command_summary: Law of the grain offering introduced
interpretation_notes: Introduces priestly regulations for the grain offering.

full_scripture: And he shall take of it his handful, of the flour of the meat offering, and of the oil thereof, and all the frankincense which is upon the meat offering, and shall burn it upon the altar for a sweet savour, even the memorial of it, unto the LORD.
speaker: God
command_summary: Burn memorial portion of grain offering
interpretation_notes: The memorial portion represents symbolic remembrance before God.

full_scripture: And the remainder thereof shall Aaron and his sons eat: with unleavened bread shall it be eaten in the holy place; in the court of the tabernacle of the congregation they shall eat it.
speaker: God
conditions: after the memorial portion is burned
command_summary: Priests eat remainder in holy place
interpretation_notes: Priestly consumption signifies sacred participation in the offering.

full_scripture: It shall not be baked with leaven. I have given it unto them for their portion of my offerings made by fire; it is most holy, as is the sin offering, and as the trespass offering.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not use leaven in grain offering
interpretation_notes: Leaven symbolizes impurity or corruption in sacrificial context.

full_scripture: All the males among the children of Aaron shall eat of it. It shall be a statute for ever in your generations concerning the offerings of the LORD made by fire: every one that toucheth them shall be holy.
speaker: God
command_summary: Only male priests may eat the offering
interpretation_notes: Emphasizes consecration and restricted access to holy offerings.

full_scripture: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,
speaker: God
command_summary: Transition to new instruction from the LORD
interpretation_notes: Marks shift from grain offering to priestly sin offering instructions.

full_scripture: This is the offering of Aaron and of his sons, which they shall offer unto the LORD in the day when he is anointed; the tenth part of an ephah of fine flour for a meat offering perpetual, half of it in the morning, and half thereof at night.
speaker: God
command_summary: Priestly daily grain offering instituted
interpretation_notes: Establishes perpetual grain offering for consecrated priests.

full_scripture: In a pan it shall be made with oil; and when it is baked, thou shalt bring it in: and the baked pieces of the meat offering shalt thou offer for a sweet savour unto the LORD.
speaker: God
command_summary: Prepare and offer baked grain offering
interpretation_notes: Details preparation method for grain offering.

full_scripture: And the priest of his sons that is anointed in his stead shall offer it: it is a statute for ever unto the LORD; it shall be wholly burnt.
speaker: God
command_summary: Anointed priest offers perpetual grain offering
interpretation_notes: Establishes continuity of priestly duty across generations.

full_scripture: For every meat offering for the priest shall be wholly burnt: it shall not be eaten.
speaker: God
command_summary: Priestly grain offering is not eaten but burned
interpretation_notes: Distinguishes priestly offering from other grain offerings.

full_scripture: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,
speaker: God
command_summary: Transition to sin offering law
interpretation_notes: Introduces sin offering regulations following grain offering laws.

full_scripture: Speak unto Aaron and to his sons, saying, This is the law of the sin offering: In the place where the burnt offering is killed shall the sin offering be killed before the LORD: it is most holy.
speaker: God
command_summary: Kill sin offering in designated place
interpretation_notes: Emphasizes sanctity and proper location of sin offering.

full_scripture: The priest that offereth it for sin shall eat it: in the holy place shall it be eaten, in the court of the tabernacle of the congregation.
speaker: God
command_summary: Priest eats sin offering in holy place
interpretation_notes: Priestly consumption signifies bearing of iniquity within sacred context.

full_scripture: Whatsoever shall touch the flesh thereof shall be holy: and when there is sprinkled of the blood thereof upon any garment, thou shalt wash that whereon it was sprinkled in the holy place.
speaker: God
command_summary: Maintain holiness and wash contaminated garments
interpretation_notes: Holiness is transferred through contact requiring ritual cleansing.

full_scripture: But the earthen vessel wherein it is sodden shall be broken: and if it be sodden in a brazen pot, it shall be both scoured, and rinsed in water.
speaker: God
command_summary: Destroy or cleanse vessels used for sin offering
interpretation_notes: Objects absorb ritual impurity requiring destruction or cleansing.

full_scripture: All the males among the priests shall eat thereof: it is most holy.
speaker: God
command_summary: Only male priests may eat sin offering
interpretation_notes: Restricts participation to consecrated male priests.

full_scripture: And no sin offering, whereof any of the blood is brought into the tabernacle of the congregation to reconcile withal in the holy place, shall be eaten: it shall be burnt in the fire.
speaker: God
conditions: when blood is brought into the tabernacle
command_summary: Do not eat sin offering with blood brought inside
interpretation_notes: Distinguishes offerings based on ritual blood placement.

full_scripture: Likewise this is the law of the trespass offering: it is most holy.
speaker: God
command_summary: Law of the trespass offering is most holy
interpretation_notes: Introduces the holiness status of the trespass offering.

full_scripture: In the place where they kill the burnt offering shall they kill the trespass offering: and the blood thereof shall he sprinkle round about upon the altar.
speaker: God
command_summary: Kill and sprinkle blood of trespass offering
interpretation_notes: Same ritual space as burnt offering reinforces shared atonement system.

full_scripture: And he shall offer of it all the fat thereof; the rump, and the fat that covereth the inwards.
speaker: God
command_summary: Offer all fat portions
interpretation_notes: Fat portions are reserved for God as symbolic best portions.

full_scripture: And the two kidneys, and the fat that is on them, which is by the flanks, and the caul above the liver, with the kidneys, it shall he take away:
speaker: God
command_summary: Remove kidneys and fat portions
interpretation_notes: Internal organs represent symbolic removal of impurity.

full_scripture: And the priest shall burn them upon the altar for an offering made by fire unto the LORD: it is a trespass offering.
speaker: God
command_summary: Burn the trespass offering on the altar
interpretation_notes: Burnt offering signifies full consecration and atonement.

full_scripture: Every male among the priests shall eat thereof: it shall be eaten in the holy place: it is most holy.
speaker: God
command_summary: Priests must eat the offering in a holy place
interpretation_notes: The consumption of holy portions is restricted to consecrated male priests.

full_scripture: As the sin offering is, so is the trespass offering: there is one law for them: the priest that maketh atonement therewith shall have it.
speaker: God
command_summary: Sin and guilt offerings share the same law
interpretation_notes: Establishes equivalence between sin and guilt offerings in priestly rights.

full_scripture: And the priest that offereth any man's burnt offering, even the priest shall have to himself the skin of the burnt offering which he hath offered.
speaker: God
command_summary: Priest receives the skin of the burnt offering
interpretation_notes: Specifies priestly compensation from offerings.

full_scripture: And all the meat offering that is baken in the oven, and all that is dressed in the fryingpan, and in the pan, shall be the priest's that offereth it.
speaker: God
command_summary: Priests receive cooked grain offerings
interpretation_notes: Defines distribution of grain offerings to priests.

full_scripture: And every meat offering, mingled with oil, and dry, shall all the sons of Aaron have, one as much as another.
speaker: God
command_summary: All priests share grain offerings equally
interpretation_notes: Ensures equitable distribution among priestly families.

full_scripture: This is the law of the peace offering which is offered unto the LORD.
speaker: God
command_summary: Introduction to peace offering law
interpretation_notes: Marks transition from burnt offerings to peace offerings.

full_scripture: Thanksgiving offerings must be accompanied by unleavened cakes mixed with oil, wafers, and fine flour cakes.
speaker: God
command_summary: Bring unleavened bread with thanksgiving offering
interpretation_notes: Emphasizes completeness of thanksgiving worship expression.

full_scripture: The offering must also include leavened bread along with the sacrifice of thanksgiving.
speaker: God
command_summary: Include leavened bread in thanksgiving offering
interpretation_notes: Leavened bread is uniquely permitted here, unlike other offerings.

full_scripture: One portion of each offering is to be presented as a heave offering to the LORD.
speaker: God
command_summary: Present a portion as heave offering
interpretation_notes: Designates priestly portion from thanksgiving offerings.

full_scripture: The flesh of the thanksgiving sacrifice must be eaten on the same day it is offered.
speaker: God
command_summary: Eat thanksgiving offering the same day
interpretation_notes: Emphasizes immediacy and communal participation.

full_scripture: If the sacrifice is a vow or voluntary offering, it may be eaten on the day or the next day.
speaker: God
command_summary: Vow offerings may be eaten over two days
interpretation_notes: Distinguishes time rules between thanksgiving and vow offerings.

full_scripture: Any remaining flesh must be burned on the third day.
speaker: God
conditions: if not eaten within allowed time
command_summary: Burn leftovers on third day
interpretation_notes: Prevents corruption or improper consumption of sacred meat.

full_scripture: If any flesh is eaten on the third day, the offering will not be accepted.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not eat on third day
interpretation_notes: Violating timing invalidates the sacrifice.

full_scripture: And the flesh that toucheth any unclean thing shall not be eaten; it shall be burnt with fire: and as for the flesh, all that be clean shall eat thereof.
speaker: God
conditions: if the flesh touches any unclean thing
command_summary: Do not eat unclean-contaminated sacrificial meat; burn it
interpretation_notes: Maintains ritual purity boundaries for sacrificial meals.

full_scripture: But the soul that eateth of the flesh of the sacrifice of peace offerings, that pertain unto the LORD, having his uncleanness upon him, even that soul shall be cut off from his people.
speaker: God
conditions: if a person eats peace offering while unclean
command_summary: Do not eat peace offerings while unclean
interpretation_notes: Uncleanness while partaking of sacred food results in covenant exclusion.

full_scripture: Moreover the soul that shall touch any unclean thing, as the uncleanness of man, or any unclean beast, or any abominable unclean thing, and eat of the flesh of the sacrifice of peace offerings, even that soul shall be cut off from his people.
speaker: God
conditions: if a person touches uncleanness and eats peace offering
command_summary: Do not eat peace offerings after touching uncleanness
interpretation_notes: Emphasizes separation between holiness and ritual impurity.

full_scripture: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,
speaker: God
command_summary: Introduction to fat and blood prohibition
interpretation_notes: Marks transition to dietary holiness regulations.

full_scripture: Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, Ye shall eat no manner of fat, of ox, or of sheep, or of goat.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not eat fat of ox, sheep, or goat
interpretation_notes: Fat is reserved for God in sacrificial context.

full_scripture: And the fat of the beast that dieth of itself, and the fat of that which is torn with beasts, may be used in any other use: but ye shall in no wise eat of it.
speaker: God
conditions: if the animal died naturally or was torn
command_summary: Do not eat fat from dead or torn animals
interpretation_notes: Distinguishes permissible use from ritual consumption.

full_scripture: For whosoever eateth the fat of the beast, of which men offer an offering made by fire unto the LORD, even the soul that eateth it shall be cut off from his people.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not eat sacrificial fat
interpretation_notes: Violation results in covenant exclusion.

full_scripture: Moreover ye shall eat no manner of blood, whether it be of fowl or of beast, in any of your dwellings.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not eat blood
interpretation_notes: Blood represents life and is reserved for atonement.

full_scripture: Whatsoever soul it be that eateth any manner of blood, even that soul shall be cut off from his people.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not eat blood under any circumstance
interpretation_notes: Eating blood is strictly prohibited due to its life-bearing significance.

full_scripture: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,
speaker: God
command_summary: Transition to peace offering portions
interpretation_notes: Introduces priestly portion regulations.

full_scripture: Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, He that offereth the sacrifice of his peace offerings unto the LORD shall bring his oblation unto the LORD of the sacrifice of his peace offerings.
speaker: God
command_summary: Bring peace offering properly to the LORD
interpretation_notes: Establishes proper presentation of peace offerings.

full_scripture: His own hands shall bring the offerings of the LORD made by fire; the fat with the breast, it shall he bring, that the breast may be waved for a wave offering before the LORD.
speaker: God
command_summary: Bring fat and breast for wave offering
interpretation_notes: Introduces wave offering ritual for priestly portions.

full_scripture: And the priest shall burn the fat upon the altar: but the breast shall be Aaron's and his sons'.
speaker: God
command_summary: Priests burn fat; receive breast portion
interpretation_notes: Distinguishes altar portion from priestly consumption.

full_scripture: And the right shoulder shall ye give unto the priest for an heave offering of the sacrifices of your peace offerings.
speaker: God
command_summary: Give right shoulder to the priest
interpretation_notes: Establishes priestly heave offering portion.

full_scripture: He among the sons of Aaron, that offereth the blood of the peace offerings, and the fat, shall have the right shoulder for his part.
speaker: God
command_summary: Officiating priest receives right shoulder
interpretation_notes: Rewards officiating priest for service.

full_scripture: For the wave breast and the heave shoulder have I taken of the children of Israel from off the sacrifices of their peace offerings, and have given them unto Aaron the priest and unto his sons by a statute for ever.
speaker: God
command_summary: Establish priestly wave and heave portions
interpretation_notes: Defines perpetual priestly entitlement to specific portions.

full_scripture: This is the portion of the anointing of Aaron, and of the anointing of his sons, out of the offerings of the LORD made by fire, in the day when he presented them to minister unto the LORD in the priest's office;
speaker: God
command_summary: Define priestly ordination portion
interpretation_notes: Connects sacrificial portions to priestly consecration.

full_scripture: Which the LORD commanded to be given them of the children of Israel, in the day that he anointed them, by a statute for ever throughout their generations.
speaker: God
command_summary: Perpetual statute for priestly portions
interpretation_notes: Emphasizes permanence of priestly provisions.

full_scripture: This is the law of the burnt offering, of the meat offering, and of the sin offering, and of the trespass offering, and of the consecrations, and of the sacrifice of the peace offerings;
speaker: God
command_summary: Summary of sacrificial laws
interpretation_notes: Concludes the sacrificial law sections.

full_scripture: Which the LORD commanded Moses in mount Sinai, in the day that he commanded the children of Israel to offer their oblations unto the LORD, in the wilderness of Sinai.
speaker: God
command_summary: Mosaic origin of sacrificial laws
interpretation_notes: Anchors sacrificial system in Sinai revelation.

full_scripture: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,
speaker: God
command_summary: God initiates the ordination instructions
interpretation_notes: Introduction to the ordination ritual establishing Aaronic priesthood.

full_scripture: Take Aaron and his sons with him, and the garments, and the anointing oil, and a bullock for the sin offering, and two rams, and a basket of unleavened bread;
speaker: God
command_summary: Gather Aaron, his sons, and sacrificial materials
interpretation_notes: Selection of persons and materials necessary for consecration.

full_scripture: And gather thou all the congregation together unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation. And Moses did as the LORD commanded him; and the assembly was gathered together.
speaker: God
command_summary: Assemble the congregation at the tabernacle
interpretation_notes: Public nature of priestly consecration emphasized.

full_scripture: And Moses said unto the congregation, This is the thing which the LORD commanded to be done.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Moses announces divine command
interpretation_notes: Moses functions as mediator of divine instruction.

full_scripture: And Moses brought Aaron and his sons, and washed them with water.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Wash Aaron and his sons
interpretation_notes: Ritual washing symbolizes purification before consecration.

full_scripture: And he put upon him the coat, girded him with the girdle, clothed him with the robe, and put the ephod upon him...
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Clothe Aaron in priestly garments
interpretation_notes: Priestly identity is established through sacred garments.

full_scripture: And he put the breastplate upon him: also he put in the breastplate the Urim and the Thummim.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Place breastplate with Urim and Thummim
interpretation_notes: Establishes divine guidance symbol within priestly office.

full_scripture: And he put the mitre upon his head; also upon the mitre, even upon his forefront, did he put the golden plate, the holy crown.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Place priestly headgear and holy crown
interpretation_notes: Marks sanctification and authority of the high priest.

full_scripture: And Moses took the anointing oil, and anointed the tabernacle and all that was therein, and sanctified them.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Anoint and sanctify the tabernacle
interpretation_notes: Sets apart sacred space for divine presence.

full_scripture: And he sprinkled thereof upon the altar seven times, and anointed the altar and all his vessels.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Anoint altar and vessels
interpretation_notes: Completes sanctification of worship instruments.

full_scripture: And he poured of the anointing oil upon Aaron’s head, and anointed him, to sanctify him.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Anoint Aaron as high priest
interpretation_notes: Marks formal installation of high priest.

full_scripture: And Moses brought Aaron’s sons, and put coats upon them, and girded them with girdles.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Clothe Aaron’s sons as priests
interpretation_notes: Establishes priestly identity for Aaron’s sons.

full_scripture: And he brought the bullock for the sin offering: and Aaron and his sons laid their hands upon the head of the bullock for the sin offering.
speaker: God
command_summary: Lay hands on sin offering bullock
interpretation_notes: Initiates atonement process for Aaron and his sons.

full_scripture: And he slew it; and Moses took the blood, and put it upon the horns of the altar round about, and purified the altar.
speaker: God
command_summary: Apply blood to altar for purification
interpretation_notes: The altar itself is consecrated through blood purification.

full_scripture: And he took all the fat that was upon the inwards, and the caul above the liver, and the two kidneys, and their fat, and Moses burned it upon the altar.
speaker: God
command_summary: Burn designated fat portions on altar
interpretation_notes: Selective burning of fat represents offering the best portions to God.

full_scripture: But the bullock, and his hide, his flesh, and his dung, he burnt with fire without the camp; as the LORD commanded Moses.
speaker: God
conditions: after sin offering fat is burned
command_summary: Burn remaining sin offering outside the camp
interpretation_notes: Removal outside the camp symbolizes removal of sin from the community.

full_scripture: And he brought the ram for the burnt offering: and Aaron and his sons laid their hands upon the head of the ram.
speaker: God
command_summary: Lay hands on burnt offering ram
interpretation_notes: Burnt offering signifies total consecration of priests.

full_scripture: And he killed it; and Moses sprinkled the blood upon the altar round about.
speaker: God
command_summary: Sprinkle ram blood on altar
interpretation_notes: Blood application continues consecration process.

full_scripture: And he cut the ram into pieces; and Moses burned the head, and the pieces, and the fat.
speaker: God
command_summary: Cut and burn ram pieces
interpretation_notes: Burnt offering completed in ordered ritual sequence.

full_scripture: And he washed the inwards and the legs in water; and Moses burnt the whole ram upon the altar.
speaker: God
conditions: before full consumption of burnt offering
command_summary: Wash and burn entire ram
interpretation_notes: Complete offering symbolizes total dedication.

full_scripture: And he brought the other ram, the ram of consecration: and Aaron and his sons laid their hands upon the head of the ram.
speaker: God
command_summary: Present ram of consecration and lay hands on it
interpretation_notes: Second ram signifies consecration, distinct from sin offering ram.

full_scripture: And he slew it; and Moses took of the blood of it, and put it upon the tip of Aaron's right ear, and upon the thumb of his right hand, and upon the great toe of his right foot.
speaker: God
command_summary: Apply blood to ear, hand, and foot
interpretation_notes: Blood marks total consecration of hearing, acting, and walking.

full_scripture: And he brought Aaron's sons, and Moses put of the blood upon the tip of their right ear, and upon the thumbs of their right hands, and upon the great toes of their right feet.
speaker: God
command_summary: Apply blood to Aaron’s sons
interpretation_notes: Priestly sons share identical consecration pattern with Aaron.

full_scripture: And he took the fat, and the rump, and all the fat that was upon the inwards, and the caul above the liver, and the two kidneys, and their fat, and the right shoulder:
speaker: God
command_summary: Take portions of the ram for offering
interpretation_notes: Specific sacrificial parts are designated for consecration ritual.

full_scripture: And out of the basket of unleavened bread, that was before the LORD, he took one unleavened cake, and a cake of oiled bread, and one wafer, and put them on the fat, and upon the right shoulder:
speaker: God
command_summary: Present unleavened bread offerings
interpretation_notes: Bread offerings accompany meat portions in consecration ritual.

full_scripture: And he put all upon Aaron's hands, and upon his sons' hands, and waved them for a wave offering before the LORD.
speaker: God
command_summary: Wave the offering before the LORD
interpretation_notes: Wave offering signifies presentation and dedication to God.

full_scripture: And Moses took them from off their hands, and burnt them on the altar upon the burnt offering: they were consecrations for a sweet savour: it is an offering made by fire unto the LORD.
speaker: God
command_summary: Burn consecration offerings on altar
interpretation_notes: Consecration offerings are fully consumed as pleasing aroma.

full_scripture: And Moses took the breast, and waved it for a wave offering before the LORD: for of the ram of consecration it was Moses' part; as the LORD commanded Moses.
speaker: God
command_summary: Wave breast portion as Moses’ share
interpretation_notes: Moses receives designated portion according to divine instruction.

full_scripture: And Moses took of the anointing oil, and of the blood which was upon the altar, and sprinkled it upon Aaron, and upon his garments, and upon his sons, and upon his sons' garments with him; and sanctified Aaron, and his garments, and his sons, and his sons' garments with him.
speaker: God
command_summary: Anoint and sanctify Aaron and sons
interpretation_notes: Oil and blood together signify complete sanctification.

full_scripture: And Moses said unto Aaron and his sons, Boil the flesh at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation: and there eat it with the bread that is in the basket of consecrations, as I commanded, saying, Aaron and his sons shall eat it.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Eat consecration meal at tabernacle entrance
interpretation_notes: Sacred meal seals ordination covenant.

full_scripture: And that which remaineth of the flesh and of the bread shall ye burn with fire.
speaker: God
command_summary: Burn remaining consecration food
interpretation_notes: Nothing holy is left to common use.

full_scripture: And ye shall not go out of the door of the tabernacle of the congregation in seven days, until the days of your consecration be at an end: for seven days shall he consecrate you.
speaker: God
command_summary: Remain at tabernacle for seven days
interpretation_notes: Separation period completes ordination process.

full_scripture: As he hath done this day, so the LORD hath commanded to do, to make an atonement for you.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Confirm ordination command for atonement
interpretation_notes: Moses confirms divine mandate for ordination ritual.

full_scripture: Therefore shall ye abide at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation day and night seven days, and keep the charge of the LORD, that ye die not: for so I am commanded.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Guard the tabernacle for seven days
interpretation_notes: Severe holiness requirement emphasizes life-or-death seriousness.

full_scripture: So Aaron and his sons did all things which the LORD commanded by the hand of Moses.
speaker: Narrator
command_summary: Aaron and sons obeyed all commands
interpretation_notes: Completion statement confirming full obedience to divine command.

full_scripture: And it came to pass on the eighth day, that Moses called Aaron and his sons, and the elders of Israel;
speaker: Moses
conditions: on the eighth day of ordination
command_summary: Call Aaron, his sons, and the elders
interpretation_notes: Transition from ordination to inaugural priestly service.

full_scripture: And he said unto Aaron, Take thee a young calf for a sin offering, and a ram for a burnt offering, without blemish, and offer them before the LORD.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Offer sin and burnt offerings
interpretation_notes: Establishes dual offering structure: sin offering + burnt offering.

full_scripture: And unto the children of Israel thou shalt speak, saying, Take ye a kid of the goats for a sin offering; and a calf and a lamb, both of the first year, without blemish, for a burnt offering;
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Bring sin and burnt offerings for Israel
interpretation_notes: Includes both national and priestly offerings.

full_scripture: And they brought that which Moses commanded before the tabernacle of the congregation: and all the congregation drew near and stood before the LORD.
speaker: Narrator
command_summary: Israel brings offerings before the LORD
interpretation_notes: Assembly gathers for first priestly mediation.

full_scripture: And Moses said unto Aaron, Go unto the altar, and offer thy sin offering, and thy burnt offering, and make an atonement for thyself, and for the people.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Make atonement for self and people
interpretation_notes: Priest must first be cleansed before mediating for others.

full_scripture: Aaron therefore went unto the altar, and slew the calf of the sin offering, which was for himself.
speaker: Narrator
command_summary: Aaron performs sin offering
interpretation_notes: First priestly action after ordination.

full_scripture: And he slew the burnt offering; and Aaron's sons presented unto him the blood, which he sprinkled round about upon the altar.
speaker: Narrator
command_summary: Burnt offering blood ritual performed
interpretation_notes: Priestly mediation established through blood application.

full_scripture: And he brought the people's offering, and took the goat, which was the sin offering for the people, and slew it, and offered it for sin.
speaker: Narrator
command_summary: Offer sin sacrifice for the people
interpretation_notes: Transition from priestly self-atonement to communal atonement.

full_scripture: And he brought the meat offering, and took an handful thereof, and burnt it upon the altar, beside the burnt sacrifice of the morning.
speaker: Narrator
command_summary: Present grain offering
interpretation_notes: Worship includes both sin and grain offerings.

full_scripture: And Aaron lifted up his hand toward the people, and blessed them, and came down from offering of the sin offering, and the burnt offering, and peace offerings.
speaker: Narrator
command_summary: Aaron blesses the people
interpretation_notes: Priestly ministry culminates in blessing the people.

full_scripture: And Moses and Aaron went into the tabernacle of the congregation, and came out, and blessed the people: and the glory of the LORD appeared unto all the people.
speaker: Narrator
conditions: after completion of first priestly service
command_summary: Glory of the LORD appears
interpretation_notes: Divine approval is publicly manifested.

full_scripture: And there came a fire out from before the LORD, and consumed upon the altar the burnt offering and the fat: which when all the people saw, they shouted, and fell on their faces.
speaker: Narrator
command_summary: Fire consumes the offering
interpretation_notes: God's acceptance is demonstrated through fire from heaven.

full_scripture: And Nadab and Abihu, the sons of Aaron, took either of them his censer, and put fire therein, and put incense thereon, and offered strange fire before the LORD, which he commanded them not.
speaker: narrator
conditions: during priestly service in the tabernacle
command_summary: Unauthorized offering of strange fire
interpretation_notes: Priestly innovation in worship violates commanded holiness standards.

full_scripture: And there went out fire from the LORD, and devoured them, and they died before the LORD.
speaker: narrator
command_summary: Divine judgment consumes Nadab and Abihu
interpretation_notes: God’s holiness is demonstrated through immediate judgment on improper worship.

full_scripture: Then Moses said unto Aaron, This is it that the LORD spake, saying, I will be sanctified in them that come nigh me, and before all the people I will be glorified.
speaker: Moses
conditions: after judgment on Nadab and Abihu
command_summary: God must be sanctified among His priests
interpretation_notes: Moses interprets the event as validation of God’s holiness standards.

full_scripture: And Moses called Mishael and Elzaphan, the sons of Uzziel the uncle of Aaron, and said unto them, Come near, carry your brethren from before the sanctuary out of the camp.
speaker: Moses
conditions: after priestly death in the sanctuary
command_summary: Remove the bodies from the sanctuary
interpretation_notes: Removal maintains sanctity of the tabernacle area.

full_scripture: And Moses said unto Aaron, and unto Eleazar and unto Ithamar, his sons, Uncover not your heads, neither rend your clothes; lest ye die, and lest wrath come upon all the people.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Do not mourn in the usual way
interpretation_notes: Priests must prioritize sanctity of office over personal mourning rituals.

full_scripture: And the LORD spake unto Aaron, saying, Do not drink wine nor strong drink, thou, nor thy sons with thee, when ye go into the tabernacle of the congregation, lest ye die.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not drink wine before serving in the tabernacle
interpretation_notes: Clarity of mind is required for priestly discernment and service.

full_scripture: And Moses spake unto Aaron, and unto Eleazar and Ithamar, his sons, Take the meat offering that remaineth... and eat it without leaven beside the altar.
speaker: Moses
command_summary: Consume remaining holy offerings
interpretation_notes: Priests are responsible for consuming portions of the offerings as commanded.

full_scripture: And Moses diligently sought the goat of the sin offering... and was angry with Eleazar and Ithamar.
speaker: Moses
conditions: after irregular handling of sin offering
command_summary: Inquiry about sin offering handling
interpretation_notes: Moses investigates possible deviation from sacrificial law.

full_scripture: And Aaron said unto Moses, Behold, this day have they offered their sin offering... and such things have befallen me: and if I had eaten the sin offering today, should it have been accepted?
speaker: Aaron
command_summary: Defense of priestly decision regarding offerings
interpretation_notes: Aaron appeals to appropriateness of mourning context affecting ritual participation.

full_scripture: Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, These are the beasts which ye shall eat among all the beasts that are on the earth.
speaker: God
command_summary: Define clean animals that may be eaten
interpretation_notes: Establishes foundational dietary boundaries for Israel.

full_scripture: Whatsoever parteth the hoof, and is clovenfooted, and cheweth the cud, among the beasts, that shall ye eat.
speaker: God
conditions: animals must have split hoof and chew cud
command_summary: Only animals with split hoof and cud chewing are clean
interpretation_notes: Physical markers define ritual dietary acceptability.

full_scripture: Nevertheless these shall ye not eat of them that chew the cud, or of them that divide the hoof...
speaker: God
conditions: exception list for clean animal rules
command_summary: Certain cud-chewing animals are still unclean
interpretation_notes: Exceptions prevent purely mechanical classification.

full_scripture: These shall ye eat of all that are in the waters: whatsoever hath fins and scales...
speaker: God
conditions: aquatic animals must have fins and scales
command_summary: Only fish with fins and scales are clean
interpretation_notes: Aquatic classification mirrors land animal distinctions.

full_scripture: And all that have not fins and scales... shall be an abomination unto you.
speaker: God
conditions: aquatic creatures without fins and scales
command_summary: Unclean sea creatures are forbidden
interpretation_notes: Defines dietary boundaries through negative classification.

full_scripture: And these are they which ye shall have in abomination among the fowls...
speaker: God
command_summary: List of unclean birds
interpretation_notes: Bird classification is mostly descriptive rather than procedural.

full_scripture: And the vulture, and the kite after his kind;
speaker: God
command_summary: Regard the vulture and kite as unclean
interpretation_notes: These birds are included among prohibited birds under Mosaic dietary law.

full_scripture: Every raven after his kind;
speaker: God
command_summary: Regard ravens as unclean
interpretation_notes: Ravens are grouped among birds prohibited for consumption.

full_scripture: And the owl, and the night hawk, and the cuckow, and the hawk after his kind,
speaker: God
command_summary: Regard owls and hawks as unclean
interpretation_notes: Several predatory or nocturnal birds are classified as unclean.

full_scripture: And the little owl, and the cormorant, and the great owl,
speaker: God
command_summary: Regard certain owls and cormorants as unclean
interpretation_notes: Birds associated with scavenging or uncleanness are prohibited.

full_scripture: And the swan, and the pelican, and the gier eagle,
speaker: God
command_summary: Regard swans and pelicans as unclean
interpretation_notes: These birds are identified among prohibited species within dietary restrictions.

full_scripture: And the stork, the heron after her kind, and the lapwing, and the bat.
speaker: God
command_summary: Regard storks, herons, and bats as unclean
interpretation_notes: The list of prohibited flying creatures concludes with additional birds and the bat.

full_scripture: All fowls that creep, going upon all four, shall be an abomination unto you.
speaker: God
command_summary: Regard creeping winged creatures as abominable
interpretation_notes: Flying insects or winged creeping things are broadly prohibited unless specifically exempted.

full_scripture: Yet these may ye eat of every flying creeping thing that goeth upon all four, which have legs above their feet, to leap withal upon the earth;
speaker: God
conditions: if the flying creeping thing has leaping legs
command_summary: Eat approved leaping flying insects
interpretation_notes: An exception is made for certain locust-like insects suitable for consumption.

full_scripture: Even these of them ye may eat; the locust after his kind, and the bald locust after his kind, and the beetle after his kind, and the grasshopper after his kind.
speaker: God
command_summary: Eat permitted kinds of locusts and grasshoppers
interpretation_notes: Specific insect categories are approved under Mosaic dietary distinctions.

full_scripture: But all other flying creeping things, which have four feet, shall be an abomination unto you.
speaker: God
conditions: if not among the approved insect categories
command_summary: Reject all other flying creeping things
interpretation_notes: The general prohibition remains in force outside the explicitly permitted exceptions.

full_scripture: And for these ye shall be unclean: whosoever toucheth the carcase of them shall be unclean until the even.
speaker: God
conditions: after touching the carcass of an unclean creature
command_summary: Touching unclean carcasses causes ritual uncleanness
interpretation_notes: The uncleanness lasts until evening, reflecting temporary ceremonial impurity.

full_scripture: And whosoever beareth ought of the carcase of them shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the even.
speaker: God
conditions: when carrying the carcass of an unclean animal
command_summary: Wash clothes after carrying unclean carcasses
interpretation_notes: Purification procedures accompany contact with ritually unclean carcasses.

full_scripture: The carcases of every beast which divideth the hoof, and is not clovenfooted, nor cheweth the cud, are unclean unto you: every one that toucheth them shall be unclean.
speaker: God
command_summary: Touching prohibited beast carcasses causes uncleanness
interpretation_notes: Animals lacking the required characteristics are ceremonially unclean.

full_scripture: And whatsoever goeth upon his paws, among all manner of beasts that go on all four, those are unclean unto you: whoso toucheth their carcase shall be unclean until the even.
speaker: God
conditions: upon touching the carcass of pawed animals
command_summary: Pawed animals are ceremonially unclean
interpretation_notes: This category includes four-footed animals that move on paws rather than divided hooves.

full_scripture: And he that beareth the carcase of them shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the even: they are unclean unto you.
speaker: God
command_summary: Wash after carrying unclean pawed carcasses
interpretation_notes: Ceremonial washing is consistently required after contact with unclean carcasses.

full_scripture: These also shall be unclean unto you among the creeping things that creep upon the earth; the weasel, and the mouse, and the tortoise after his kind.
speaker: God
conditions: regarding creeping things upon the earth
command_summary: Certain creeping creatures are unclean
interpretation_notes: Specific creeping animals are identified as ritually defiling.

full_scripture: And the ferret, and the chameleon, and the lizard, and the snail, and the mole.
speaker: God
conditions: as part of the creeping unclean creatures list
command_summary: Additional creeping creatures are unclean
interpretation_notes: The exact zoological identities of some listed creatures are debated.

full_scripture: These are unclean to you among all that creep: whosoever doth touch them, when they be dead, shall be unclean until the even.
speaker: God
conditions: when touching the carcass of unclean creeping things
command_summary: Avoid contact with dead unclean creeping things
interpretation_notes: Contact with carcasses of unclean creatures results in temporary ceremonial uncleanness.

full_scripture: And upon whatsoever any of them, when they are dead, doth fall, it shall be unclean; whether it be any vessel of wood, or raiment, or skin, or sack.
speaker: God
conditions: when an unclean carcass falls upon an object
command_summary: Treat contaminated objects as unclean
interpretation_notes: Ceremonial impurity extends from carcasses to objects they contact.

full_scripture: It must be put into water, and it shall be unclean until the even; so it shall be cleansed.
speaker: God
conditions: after an item becomes ceremonially unclean
command_summary: Wash contaminated items with water
interpretation_notes: Water purification rituals restore ceremonial cleanness after contamination.

full_scripture: And every earthen vessel, whereinto any of them falleth, whatsoever is in it shall be unclean; and ye shall break it.
speaker: God
conditions: when an unclean carcass falls into an earthen vessel
command_summary: Break contaminated earthen vessels
interpretation_notes: Porous earthenware could not be ritually purified and therefore had to be destroyed.

full_scripture: Of all meat which may be eaten, that on which such water cometh shall be unclean.
speaker: God
conditions: when food is contaminated by unclean contact
command_summary: Regard contaminated food as unclean
interpretation_notes: Impurity spreads through contaminated liquids and food.

full_scripture: And every thing whereupon any part of their carcase falleth shall be unclean; whether it be oven, or ranges for pots, they shall be broken down.
speaker: God
conditions: when cooking structures are contaminated by carcasses
command_summary: Destroy contaminated ovens and pottery ranges
interpretation_notes: Objects permanently retaining impurity were to be destroyed.

full_scripture: Nevertheless a fountain or pit, wherein there is plenty of water, shall be clean: but that which toucheth their carcase shall be unclean.
speaker: God
command_summary: Flowing water sources remain clean despite carcass contact
interpretation_notes: Large or flowing water sources are treated differently from containers in purity regulations.

full_scripture: And if any part of their carcase fall upon any sowing seed which is to be sown, it shall be clean.
speaker: God
conditions: when dry seed contacts an unclean carcass
command_summary: Dry seed remains ceremonially clean
interpretation_notes: Dry seed is exempt from impurity transfer in this context.

full_scripture: But if any water be put upon the seed, and any part of their carcase fall thereon, it shall be unclean unto you.
speaker: God
conditions: when wet seed contacts an unclean carcass
command_summary: Wet contaminated seed becomes unclean
interpretation_notes: Moisture allows ceremonial impurity to transfer to seed.

full_scripture: And if any beast, of which ye may eat, die; he that toucheth the carcase thereof shall be unclean until the even.
speaker: God
conditions: when touching the carcass of a clean animal that died naturally
command_summary: Touching dead clean animals causes uncleanness
interpretation_notes: Even otherwise clean animals become sources of impurity when found dead.

full_scripture: And he that eateth of the carcase of it shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the even.
speaker: God
conditions: after eating from the carcass of a dead animal
command_summary: Wash after eating from a carcass
interpretation_notes: Washing rituals restore ceremonial cleanness after defilement.

full_scripture: And every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth shall be an abomination; it shall not be eaten.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not eat creeping things
