interpretation_notes: Establishes creeping creatures as ritually unclean for consumption.

full_scripture: Whatsoever goeth upon the belly, and whatsoever goeth upon all four, or whatsoever hath more feet among all creeping things that creep upon the earth, them ye shall not eat; for they are an abomination.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not eat creatures that crawl or multiply legs
interpretation_notes: Expands classification of unclean creatures based on movement and form.

full_scripture: Ye shall not make yourselves abominable with any creeping thing that creepeth, neither shall ye make yourselves unclean with them, that ye should be defiled thereby.
speaker: God
command_summary: Do not defile yourselves with creeping things
interpretation_notes: Focuses on personal defilement through dietary impurity.

full_scripture: For I am the LORD your God: ye shall therefore sanctify yourselves, and ye shall be holy; for I am holy: neither shall ye defile yourselves with any manner of creeping thing.
speaker: God
command_summary: Be holy because God is holy
interpretation_notes: Provides theological foundation for dietary purity laws.

full_scripture: For I am the LORD that bringeth you up out of the land of Egypt, to be your God: ye shall therefore be holy, for I am holy.
speaker: God
command_summary: Be holy because God redeemed Israel
interpretation_notes: Links holiness to covenant identity and redemption.

full_scripture: This is the law of the beasts, and of the fowl, and of every living creature that moveth in the waters, and of every creature that creepeth upon the earth:
speaker: God
command_summary: Summary of clean and unclean laws
interpretation_notes: Serves as a structural conclusion to the dietary purity section.

full_scripture: To make a difference between the unclean and the clean, and between the beast that may be eaten and the beast that may not be eaten.
speaker: God
command_summary: Distinguish between clean and unclean animals
interpretation_notes: Final verse summarizes the purpose of dietary laws: discernment between holy and common.

full_scripture: Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, If a woman have conceived seed, and born a man child: then she shall be unclean seven days; according to the days of the separation for her infirmity shall she be unclean.
speaker: God
conditions: if a woman gives birth to a male child
command_summary: Birth of a male child results in seven days of uncleanness
interpretation_notes: Ritual impurity is tied to childbirth, not moral sin.

full_scripture: And in the eighth day the flesh of his foreskin shall be circumcised.
speaker: God
conditions: on the eighth day after male birth
command_summary: Circumcise male child on the eighth day
interpretation_notes: Circumcision marks covenant inclusion under Abrahamic continuity.

full_scripture: And she shall then continue in the blood of her purifying three and thirty days; she shall touch no hallowed thing, nor come into the sanctuary, until the days of her purifying be fulfilled.
speaker: God
conditions: after childbirth of a male child
command_summary: Remain in purification period and avoid holy things
interpretation_notes: Extended purification period reflects ritual separation, not moral condemnation.

full_scripture: But if she bear a maid child, then she shall be unclean two weeks, as in her separation: and she shall continue in the blood of her purifying threescore and six days.
speaker: God
conditions: if a woman gives birth to a female child
command_summary: Female birth requires longer purification period
interpretation_notes: The text distinguishes duration of purification based on childbirth sex category.

full_scripture: And when the days of her purifying are fulfilled, for a son, or for a daughter, she shall bring a lamb of the first year for a burnt offering, and a young pigeon, or a turtledove, for a sin offering, unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, unto the priest:
speaker: God
command_summary: Bring offerings after purification
interpretation_notes: Restoration includes both burnt offering and sin offering.

full_scripture: And he shall offer it before the LORD, and make an atonement for her; and she shall be cleansed from the issue of her blood. This is the law for her that hath born a male or a female.
speaker: God
command_summary: Priest makes atonement and declares cleansing
interpretation_notes: Cleansing is completed through priestly mediation and sacrifice.
